---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Caspar Leung and Elizabeth Louie"
date: "2026-03-05"
output: html_document
---


```{r initialization}
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)

# Import data file skipping first 2 rows 
df <- read.csv("multiTimeline.csv", skip = 2, header = TRUE)

# Rename columns
colnames(df) <- c("Time", "Interest")

df$Time <- substr(df$Time, 1, 19)

# Check the first few rows
head(df)
summary(df$Interest)
```

```{r remove outlier}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  # geom_point creates the scatter plot dots
  # alpha = 0.6 makes the dots slightly transparent to show overlapping data
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest"
  )

hist(df$Interest,
     main = "Histogram of BTS Search Interest",
     xlab = "Interest")

df[which.max(df$Interest), ]

ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_point(
    data = df[df$Time == "2026-01-01", ],
    color = "red",
    size = 3
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )

df_clean <- df[df$Time != "2026-01-01", ]

ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time (Outlier Removed)",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )
```


```{r}
# Time series visualization  
bts_ts <- ts(df_clean$Interest, frequency = 12, start = c(2021,3)) 

# Check data variance 
BoxCox.lambda(bts_ts)

# Fixed skew with log transformation
bts_log <- log(bts_ts)
hist(bts_log,
     main = "Histogram of Log-Transformed BTS Interest",
     xlab = "log(Interest)")

qqnorm(bts_log)
qqline(bts_log)
```

```{r}

# Create time index
t <- 1:length(bts_ts)

# Fit least squares trend
trend_fit <- lm(as.numeric(bts_ts) ~ t)

# Plot original series with fitted linear trend
plot(bts_ts,
     main = "BTS Search Interest with Least Squares Trend",
     ylab = "Interest",
     xlab = "Time")

lines(trend_fit$fitted.values, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Plot fitted trend alone
trend_ts <- ts(trend_fit$fitted.values,
               start = c(2021, 3),
               frequency = 12)

plot(trend_ts,
     main = "Least Squares Trend Component",
     ylab = "Trend",
     xlab = "Time")

# Detrend series
detrended_ts <- bts_ts - trend_fit$fitted.values

plot(ts(detrended_ts, start = c(2021, 3), frequency = 12),
     main = "Detrended Series",
     ylab = "Detrended",
     xlab = "Time")

```


```{r}
# Estimate trend with 12-term moving average filter
hatm <- filter(bts_ts, sides = 2, c(0.5, rep(1, 11), 0.5) / 12)

# Reshape data into yearly rows, monthly columns
A <- matrix(bts_ts, ncol = 12, byrow = TRUE)
M <- matrix(hatm,  ncol = 12, byrow = TRUE)

# Estimate seasonal means
mu <- array(0, 12)

for (k in 1:6)  mu[k]  <- sum(A[2:5, k] - M[2:5, k], na.rm = TRUE) / 4
for (k in 7:12) mu[k] <- sum(A[1:4, k] - M[1:4, k], na.rm = TRUE) / 4

# Force seasonal effects to sum to zero
hats <- rep(mu - mean(mu), 5)

# Turn seasonal component into time series form
seasonal_component <- ts(hats, start = c(2021, 3), frequency = 12)

plot(seasonal_component,
     main = "Estimated Seasonal Component",
     ylab = "Seasonality",
     xlab = "Time")
```

```{r}
# Deseasonalize 
bts_adj <- bts_ts
desea <- bts_adj - seasonal_component
plot(desea,
     main = "Deseasonalized BTS Search Interest",
     ylab = "Deseasonalized",
     xlab = "Time")

# Least Squares Trend Fitting 
t <- 1:length(desea)

# Fit trend to deseasonalized series 
trend_fit <- lm(as.numeric(desea) ~ t + I(t^2))
summary(trend_fit)

trend_component <- ts(trend_fit$fitted.values,
                      start = c(2021, 3),
                      frequency = 12)

plot(desea,
     main = "Deseasonalized Series with Fitted Trend",
     ylab = "Deseasonalized",
     xlab = "Time")
lines(trend_component, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Observe residuals
smooth_resid <- desea - trend_component
```



```{r}

# Residual analysis 
acf(smooth_resid, main = "ACF of Residuals")
pacf(smooth_resid, main = "PACF of Residuals")

#ordered
```


```{r}
model_ar1 <- arima(residuals_ts, order = c(1,0,0))
model_ma1 <- arima(residuals_ts, order = c(0,0,1))
model_arma11 <- arima(residuals_ts, order = c(1,0,1))

AIC(model_ar1, model_ma1, model_arma11)

tsdiag(model_ma1)
acf(residuals(model_ma1))
summary(model_ma1)
```

```{r}
# how many future months to predict 
h <- 12 

#trend forcast 
time_index <- 1:length(bts_ts)
```
